questions.question: Current position is N43°59.1' W88°33.4'. Your destination is N44°04.8' W91°42.7'. How far are you from your destination?
Ground speed is 452 knots. How far will you travel in 17 minutes?

You are working as a fueler at an airport. A transient aircraft lands and requests to be refueled, but can only take on 78 pounds of 100LL because he is picking up a passenger that weighs 157 LBS. How many liters of fuel do you pump?
At what density altitude will a true airspeed of 142 knots equal a calibrated airspeed of 173 MPH?

You calculate a ground speed of 173 knots, and your G1000 shows a wind of 030° at 40 knots. What is your course, assuming your true airspeed is 142 knots and your wind correction angle is 9° to the left?

Checkpoint G and Checkpoint H lie on a magnetic course of 315°. Flying a magnetic heading of 323°, it takes you 16:45 to fly between them at 122 KTAS. What are the winds aloft if Checkpoint G and Checkpoint H are 78 KM apart?
How many 1-quart oil cans will it take to equal the weight of six 55-gallon drums of 100 LL avgas?
An airport has a field elevation of 3,150' MSL. With an altimeter setting of 1021 mb, what atmospheric temperature will yield a density altitude of 4,750'?

What is your radius of action with night VFR reserve fuel? Consider the following: Outbound course: 125°; Fuel: 54.5 US gallons, 6.4 liters unusable; [[NEXT_LINE]] Fuel burn: 9.6 GPH; Cruise speed: 286 KM/HR; [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds aloft: 274° at 41 knots.
How tall is a redwood tree if you are lying on the ground gazing at it from 1/4 SM away? The angle from your eyes to the top of the tree is 11.76°.
Having developed a fuel imbalance of 1,200 pounds, how long must you crossfeed to balance your fuel tanks? The total fuel burn is 6,700 pounds per hour.
You are in Sun Valley, ID (5320') and the METAR reports winds are 270° at 32 knots. Your aircraft limitation is a 10-knot tailwind. Can you accept a departure off runway 15? (variation 13° E).

Despite how you answered above, you elect to depart on runway 33. Can you clear an obstacle 10.6 NM off the departure end of the runway that is 9,151' tall? The aircraft climbs at 65 KTAS and 290 FPM.

choices.text: Yes, barely clear it by 6 feet
No, you need to climb 1361 feet more
No, you need to climb 1138 feet more

questions.correctChoice: Yes, barely clear it by 6 feet
questions.context: Referenced question 12: You are in Sun Valley, ID (5320') and the METAR reports winds are 270° at 32 knots. Your aircraft limitation is a 10-knot tailwind. Can you accept a departure off runway 15? (variation 13° E).

questions.question: The longest contiguous U.S. flight is from KMIA to KSEA at 4,383.86 KM. How long is this in feet?
Cruising over Mount Adams (12,120') at 14,500', if your altimeter is set to 30.32" Hg and the OAT is -23°F what is your true altitude?
Orbiting around Mount Adams at 9.2 SM, how many nautical miles do you cover per orbit?
The FBO fuel truck can hold 10,000 gallons. 9,956 gallons of that is usable AVGAS. If the truck is 42% full, how many liters are left in the fuel truck?

You are traveling between Oakland and San Francisco course of 229° and distance of 9.6 NM. The current wind is 310° at 15 knots and you are cruising at 93 KTAS. At what point is your time to San Francisco the same as returning to Oakland?
What is your ground speed across the bay to San Francisco?
questions.context: Referenced question 18: You are traveling between Oakland and San Francisco course of 229° and distance of 9.6 NM. The current wind is 310° at 15 knots and you are cruising at 93 KTAS. At what point is your time to San Francisco the same as returning to Oakland?

questions.question: You travel between two bearings, from 087° bearing to 122° bearing, in 42 minutes at 35 NM. What is your time to the station?

The max takeoff weight of a B737 (two-engine passenger jet) is 85,139 LBS and each engine produces 13,150 LBS of thrust. While the Challenger 300's (two-engine corporate jet) max takeoff weight is 38,850 LBS and each engine produces 6,826 LBS of thrust. How much better is the Challenger 300's thrust to weight ratio?
You are about to embark upon a bicycle ride of 104 statute miles. If your tire has a 28" diameter how many complete revolutions will your tire make during the ride?
Assuming you maintain a 16-MPH average pace what is the average RPM of your tire?
questions.context: Referenced question 22: You are about to embark upon a bicycle ride of 104 statute miles. If your tire has a 28" diameter how many complete revolutions will your tire make during the ride?

questions.question: In cruise you have leaned your six cylinder Continental to 90 pounds per hour of 100LL. Assuming this results in an air:fuel mixture of 15:1 how much air will flow through each cylinder per hour?

At high power cruise you will burn 17 GPH and cruise at 167 knots. If you pull the power back to an economy cruise fuel flow will drop to 14 GPH and your speed will fall to 151 knots. Over a 400-NM trip how much fuel will you save?

There is a hill 1.5 nautical miles off the departure end of the runway that is 800' above airport elevation. On top of this hill they have built a cell tower that is 200' tall. If you want to clear this tower by 100' what climb gradient will you need to maintain?
Based on the previous question, if you plan to climb at 90 MPH what vertical speed will this require assuming you are flying into a constant 10 MPH headwind?
questions.context: Referenced question 26: There is a hill 1.5 nautical miles off the departure end of the runway that is 800' above airport elevation. On top of this hill they have built a cell tower that is 200' tall. If you want to clear this tower by 100' what climb gradient will you need to maintain?

questions.question: You have completed your planned weight and balance and determined your aircraft should weigh 2,570 LBS with a CG of 43.6". When one of your passengers arrives they inform you their dog has to come on the trip. Fine, but it's going in the back! What will be your new CG if it only weighs 15 LBS and will be located at 87"?
Referencing the previous question, during flight the dog manages to escape and jumps up into the front seat at 36.5". Where will this put the CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 28: You have completed your planned weight and balance and determined your aircraft should weigh 2,570 LBS with a CG of 43.6". When one of your passengers arrives they inform you their dog has to come on the trip. Fine, but it's going in the back! What will be your new CG if it only weighs 15 LBS and will be located at 87"?

questions.question: You are flying a 320° heading but notice you are drifting 5° left of course. To correct you fly heading 270° but that causes you to drift 4° right. If you are maintaining 105 knots what are the winds?
Now that you have that figured out, if your desired true course was 305° what heading should you fly?
questions.context: Referenced question 30: You are flying a 320° heading but notice you are drifting 5° left of course. To correct you fly heading 270° but that causes you to drift 4° right. If you are maintaining 105 knots what are the winds?

questions.question: The critical Mach number of an aircraft is the speed at which airflow on some point of the wing reaches the speed of sound. For your aircraft it is M0.86 at the service ceiling of FL410 in a standard atmosphere. If you are flying at M0.86 in those conditions, how much does the airflow accelerate from free stream velocity to its fastest point on the wing?

You are flying a holding pattern at 18 DME, and you notice the CDI deflected 4° off the selected course while you are abeam the holding fix on the outbound leg. Assuming no wind, what is your turn radius in the holding pattern?

With the applicable FAA approvals taken care of, you are using your camera drone to take a video of your friend's takeoff in her newly restored Globe Swift. The Swift has a takeoff roll of 970', and it takes 11 seconds to reach liftoff speed. Your drone has a maximum speed of 35 MPH. You will fly the drone parallel to the runway at maximum speed to capture the takeoff. If you want the Swift to lift off exactly when it is abeam the drone, how long should your friend wait after the drone passes to begin her takeoff roll?
Referencing the previous question, if the Swift lifts off at 70 MPH, what is its average rate of acceleration during the takeoff roll?
questions.context: Referenced question 34: With the applicable FAA approvals taken care of, you are using your camera drone to take a video of your friend's takeoff in her newly restored Globe Swift. The Swift has a takeoff roll of 970', and it takes 11 seconds to reach liftoff speed. Your drone has a maximum speed of 35 MPH. You will fly the drone parallel to the runway at maximum speed to capture the takeoff. If you want the Swift to lift off exactly when it is abeam the drone, how long should your friend wait after the drone passes to begin her takeoff roll?

questions.question: You plan a 147 NM-flight using winds aloft of 030° at 28 KTS and true course of 349°. At max cruise power, your aircraft cruises at 139 KTAS and burns 10.2 GPH; at economy cruise it goes 122 KTAS and burns 7.7 GPH. If 100LL is $4.87 per gallon at your home airport, how much money do you save by cruising at economy power instead of max power?

Instead of overflying KDCU you decide to keep flying along the same course past KDCU as long as you can before returning to KCHA. Disregarding climb and descent performance, which of the following altitudes would allow you to travel the farthest distance total?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 37-39. To build hours you initially plan a flight from KCHA to KDCU, a magnetic course of 261° and distance of 89 NM. You will overfly KDCU and then return to KCHA. Your aircraft has 144 liters of usable fuel. You plan to land at KCHA with 30 minutes of fuel reserve. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: Based on the altitude chosen in the previous question, how far past KDCU can you fly?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 37-39. To build hours you initially plan a flight from KCHA to KDCU, a magnetic course of 261° and distance of 89 NM. You will overfly KDCU and then return to KCHA. Your aircraft has 144 liters of usable fuel. You plan to land at KCHA with 30 minutes of fuel reserve. questions.question: How much difference in total time airborne is there between choosing 4,500' MSL and 8,500' MSL?
